explicit_text: I worked down in Montpelier at the Pavilion as a waitress, and that old Hafer said I was the last young waitress he'd ever hire. I also know the town well because I had a picture of my daughter graduating from high school there. Montpelier is where the State House is, and I used to work there proofreading for the WPA. The town has its ups and downs, but I know a lot of people there and have been around for a long time.

The town has its ups and downs, but I know a lot of people there and have been around for a long time.

explicit_text: I married a lieutenant who was an engineer on the East Barre Dam. He was a good-looking man and the last husband I had. We had some good times together, but it didn't last long because he was too good-looking and all the women went after him. That was back when I was younger and still moving around a lot.
implicit_text: I married a lieutenant who was an engineer on that big dam project in Barre. He was a good-looking man and the last husband I had. We had some good times together, but it didn't last long because he was too good-looking and all the women went after him. That was back when I was younger and still moving around a lot.

explicit_text: One weekend, we went up to Gallagher's in Phillipsburg. We had a great time there, even though we had planned to go to Montreal but didn't get beyond Phillipsburg. It was right on the water, near Missisquoi Bay, and the breeze in the morning was fresh and clean off the lake. That trip stands out as a memorable time with Eddie and the gang.

It was right on the water, near Missisquoi Bay, and the breeze in the morning was fresh and clean off the lake. That trip stands out as a memorable time with Eddie and the gang.

explicit_text: Phillipsburg was a nice place right on the water by Missisquoi Bay. We stopped there on a trip that was supposed to take us to Montreal, but we didn't get any further. The town had a lively atmosphere, and I remember the fresh breeze blowing in the windows in the morning. It was a good spot for dancing and having fun with friends like Eddie.

explicit_text: We had planned a trip to Montreal, but we never quite made it there. We got as far as Phillipsburg and even stopped at St. John's, where the girls tried to make men out of us, but we later found out they were professionals. Montreal was the intended destination, but circumstances kept us from reaching it. Still, the trip was full of interesting experiences.

explicit_text: On that trip, we stopped to dance in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador. The girls there tried to make men out of us, but we later found out they were professionals. It was a tough joint, and not the kind of place you forget easily. That stop was part of the adventure before we headed back.

explicit_text: Phillipsburg sits right on the water at Missisquoi Bay, and I remember how the breeze blew in the window in the morning, fresh and clean off the lake. The water was dark blue with white sails out in the middle, a beautiful sight that morning even though I had a hangover. It was a peaceful and refreshing place to be during that trip.

explicit_text: I worked for the WPA on a white-collar job, making fifty-eight dollars a month, which was a far cry from what I used to earn. Before that, I was on the old chain-gang doing manual labor, which was tough but kept me in shape. The WPA job was proofreading at the State House, but sometimes there was nothing to do if my partner didn't show up. It was a hard time financially, but I was grateful to have some work during the Depression.

explicit_text: I worked at the State House in Vermont doing proofreading for the WPA. Everyone there knew me, and it was a pretty slow job since sometimes my coworker wouldn't show up and I'd just sit there. I felt a bit ashamed to be working there given the low pay and the lack of real work. Still, it was something to do during tough times when jobs were scarce.

explicit_text: Since the bank holiday in 1933, my money has been tied up and I can't get it out. I had some savings in the bank, but I ran through most of it while I was laid off and couldn't find work. The bank holiday really hit me hard because I couldn't access my funds when I needed them most. It was a tough period that made me realize how fragile financial security can be.

explicit_text: Christmas time around here mostly means drinking and dancing at the Elks Club or some other place. The same people show up every year, and you have to drink to stand it. I don't drink a lot myself because at my age it starts to show on your face and figure. The Elks Club is part of the local social scene, especially during the holidays, even if it doesn't hold much meaning for me anymore.
